steps.explain: Both terms share the factor $x^3$, so combine coefficients: $11x^3-5x^3=(11-5)x^3=6x^3$.
The exponent stays 3 — do not add exponents when combining like terms (that would only apply to multiplication).

Evaluate choices:
  • A ($16x^3$): $11+5=16$ — wrong operation (addition instead of subtraction) — eliminated.
  • B ($6x^3$): $11-5=6$, exponent 3 unchanged. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C ($6x^6$): coefficient is right but exponent doubled — eliminated.
  • D ($16x^6$): both errors — eliminated.
